untranslatable_reason: Diamond pattern detection requires geometric shape recognition (widening highs/lows followed by narrowing highs/lows forming symmetrical triangle). This requires multi-bar pattern analysis and trend line construction that cannot be expressed using point-in-time indicators. The pattern involves comparing ranges across multiple periods and detecting the transition from broadening to symmetrical phase—operations not supported by the allowed indicator set. Volume contraction during formation is captured, but the core pattern geometry is untranslatable.
translation_notes: Attempted to capture volume contraction aspect (volume downtrend noted in pattern) and used Donchian breakout as proxy for pattern completion/breakout. However, the fundamental diamond shape recognition—detecting higher highs AND lower lows in the widening phase, then lower highs AND higher lows in the symmetrical phase—requires bar-by-bar comparisons and slope detection unavailable in the allowed indicators. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT (bearish bias with 69% success rate, but works both ways). Entry/exit conditions are incomplete approximations only.
